customer_anxiety_profile: Andy is financially cautious and newly settled — managing mortgage, car payments, and multiple competing bills simultaneously. He's not hostile to solar but is protecting his bandwidth. He's been pitched by at least three solar companies recently (including a combined solar/water-softener pitch) and is likely fatigued by the category. His default posture is deferral, not rejection.
unresolved_concerns: Five-to-six-month timeline objection was bypassed by the bill-swap reframe and calendar urgency — never directly resolved. Andy may resurface this at the appointment.
Partner ('other half') was referenced but never confirmed as a participant in the appointment. Closer should verify partner availability before the sit.
Andy has seen designs from at least two other solar companies and a combined solar/water-softener pitch — competitive context is active.
sensitive_topics_raised: Financial overwhelm: mortgage, car payments, and bill management are all in flux simultaneously.
Multiple prior solar pitches at or near the same time — Andy may be fatigued or skeptical of the category.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment booked for Tuesday the 11th at 10:00 AM — in-person specialist visit.
Specialist will 'come show you' the design that was already built for Andy.
Framed as: 'if you don't like it, no harm no foul, we'll get out of your hair.'
closer_should_know: The partner was never confirmed as a participant — verify before the sit that Andy's 'other half' will be present, or this appointment is structurally exposed. Also: Andy's stated five-to-six-month deferral was bypassed, not resolved. The closer should expect that objection to resurface and should be prepared to address it directly rather than assuming Parker's bill-swap reframe closed it.
